description: You are a QA engineer preparing a small “staging” dataset that other test-runners will later consume. A single tab-separated file already exists and you must derive two new files from it using only classic column-oriented command-line tools such as cut, paste, and simple shell redirection. No scripting languages (Python, Perl, etc.) should be necessary—just 3-4 standard text-processing commands. Initial state 1. Directory: /home/user/environments/ (already created for you) 2. File: /home/user/environments/test_servers.tsv • This file is tab-delimited, four columns per row, no header line. • Exact contents (⇥ represents a single literal TAB character): web-01⇥10.10.1.11⇥8080⇥Alice api-01⇥10.10.1.21⇥9000⇥Bob db-01⇥10.10.1.31⇥5432⇥Charlie cache-01⇥10.10.1.41⇥6379⇥David mq-01⇥10.10.1.51⇥5672⇥Eve Required result A. /home/user/environments/hosts.list • Plain text, one host name per line, **no extra blank lines**. • Must contain **only** the first column from test_servers.tsv in the same order. B. /home/user/environments/host_ip_map.tsv • Tab-delimited (exactly one TAB between fields). • Each line: <host> TAB <ip> (1st and 2nd columns from the source file). • Lines must appear in the same order as the source file. • No trailing TABs or spaces; no extra blank lines. C. /home/user/environments/column_ops.log • Exactly two lines, each confirming creation of the new files: hosts.list OK host_ip_map.tsv OK • Each line must end with a single newline character (UNIX line ending). Constraints • Accomplish everything with at most 3–4 terminal commands, relying only on tools that are pre-installed on a minimal Linux system (e.g., cut, paste, cat, echo, tee). • Do not modify the original test_servers.tsv. • Make sure the two output files match the format above byte-for-byte; automated tests perform strict diff checks. • Use absolute paths in your commands or be certain you are inside /home/user/environments/ before operating on files. • After finishing, verify the files yourself and record the success lines in column_ops.log. When you are done, all three target files must exist with the exact contents described. Automated grading will diff them to the expected versions, so pay close attention to tabs, newlines, and ordering.
